name: filesystem mcp tool-selection matrix
description: Compare stock and tuned tool descriptions for common Filesystem MCP read, write, edit, list, tree, search, metadata, and move workflows.

instruction_variants.instructions: Choose the single filesystem MCP tool that should be called next. Prefer non-recursive tools for bounded directory questions. Use edit tools for small targeted changes to existing files. Use write tools only for creating or fully replacing a file.
instruction_variants.name: filesystem_host_rules

tools.name: read_file
tools.purpose: Read the complete contents of a file from the file system. Use this tool when you need to examine the contents of a single file.

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.purpose: Read the contents of multiple files simultaneously. This is more efficient than reading files one by one when you need to analyze or compare multiple files.

tools.name: write_file
tools.purpose: Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with new content.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.purpose: Make line-based edits to a text file. Each edit replaces exact line sequences with new content.

tools.name: create_directory
tools.purpose: Create a new directory or ensure a directory exists. Can create multiple nested directories in one operation.

tools.name: list_directory
tools.purpose: Get a detailed listing of all files and directories in a specified path.

tools.name: directory_tree
tools.purpose: Get a recursive tree view of files and directories as a JSON structure.

pattern.description: Pattern to match files and directories

tools.name: search_files
tools.purpose: Recursively search for files and directories matching a pattern.

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.purpose: Retrieve detailed metadata about a file or directory, including size, creation time, last modified time, permissions, and type.

tools.name: move_file
tools.purpose: Move or rename files and directories.

tools.name: list_allowed_directories
tools.purpose: Returns the list of directories that this server is allowed to access.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for multiple files, metadata-only questions, directory listings, or edits.

path.description: Exact path to the single file to read.

tools.name: read_file
tools.purpose: Read the full content of one known file.
quality_checks: Use only when one file path is known.
Do not use for metadata, existence checks, or directory listing.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to inspect the contents of a single file.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for one file, directory listings, metadata, or writes.

paths.description: Exact file paths to read together.

tools.name: read_multiple_files
tools.purpose: Read several known files in one call.
quality_checks: Use when two or more file paths are known.
Preserve all requested paths.
tools.use_when: Use to compare, summarize, or inspect multiple named files.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for small edits to existing files; use edit_file. Avoid for reading or metadata.

path.description: File path to create or replace.

tools.name: write_file
tools.purpose: Create a new file or fully replace an existing file.
quality_checks: Use only when complete content is known.
Do not use for targeted patches.
tools.use_when: Use when the user provides full file content or asks to create a new file.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for creating new files from scratch, full-file replacement, or binary files.

path.description: Existing text file path to edit.

dryRun.description: True to preview changes before applying.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.purpose: Apply targeted line-based edits to an existing text file.
quality_checks: Use for replace/patch/update requests.
Use dryRun=true when the user asks to preview.
tools.use_when: Use when the user wants to change specific lines or replace a known string in a file.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for moving, listing, searching, or writing file contents.

path.description: Directory path to create or ensure.

tools.name: create_directory
tools.purpose: Create or ensure a directory exists, including nested directories.
quality_checks: Use only for directories.
Do not create files.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to make a folder/directory.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for recursive trees or recursive filename search. Avoid for reading file contents.
tools.name: list_directory
tools.purpose: List the immediate children of one directory.
quality_checks: Use for bounded one-level listings.
Prefer over directory_tree when recursion is not requested.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks what is in a directory or whether a top-level entry exists.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for simple one-level listings or filename search by pattern.
tools.name: directory_tree
tools.purpose: Return a recursive directory tree as structured JSON.
quality_checks: Use only when the user asks for recursive structure/tree.
Avoid on very large roots unless bounded.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for a tree view or full recursive structure.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for known exact paths, one-level listings, file contents, or metadata-only questions.

pattern.description: Filename or directory-name pattern to match.

excludePatterns.description: Patterns to skip during recursive search.

tools.name: search_files
tools.purpose: Recursively find files or directories by name/pattern.
quality_checks: Use only when recursive search is needed.
Include excludePatterns for large noisy directories when known.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to find files/directories matching a pattern and the exact location is unknown.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user wants file contents, directory contents, or to change the file.

path.description: File or directory path for metadata.

tools.name: get_file_info
tools.purpose: Return metadata for one file or directory without reading contents.
quality_checks: Use for size, timestamps, permissions, and type.
Do not read file contents.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for size, modified time, permissions, or whether a path is file or directory.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for copying, editing contents, or creating directories.
tools.name: move_file
tools.purpose: Move or rename one file or directory.
quality_checks: Use source and destination exactly.
Do not use when destination already exists unless behavior is specified.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to move or rename a file or directory.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for any specific file operation when the allowed root is already known.
tools.name: list_allowed_directories
tools.purpose: List filesystem roots the MCP server can access.
quality_checks: Use before file operations when access roots are unknown.
Do not use as a directory listing.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks what directories are accessible or when no allowed root is known.

cases.name: read several known files
cases.task: Read /project/README.md, /project/package.json, and /project/CHANGELOG.md so I can compare them.

cases.name: create new file with full content
cases.task: Create /project/notes/new-plan.md with the full content I provided.

cases.name: targeted line edit
cases.task: In /project/config.yaml, replace the line timeout: 30 with timeout: 60 without rewriting the whole file.

cases.name: list immediate directory children
cases.task: List the immediate files and folders directly inside /project/src. Do not recurse.

cases.name: recursive tree view
cases.task: Show a recursive tree view of /project/src.

cases.name: recursive filename search
cases.task: Find all files matching *.test.ts somewhere under /project.

cases.name: get file metadata
cases.task: Get the size and last modified time for /project/build/output.zip without reading its contents.

cases.name: list accessible roots
cases.task: What filesystem directories is this MCP server allowed to access?
